CheckTitle: Ensure agent identities do not have overly broad permission scope
Description: Flags agent identities that have 5 or more application permission grants, indicating an overly broad scope of access that violates least-privilege principles.
Risk: An agent with many application permissions has a large blast radius. If compromised, the attacker gains access to multiple resource types across the tenant.

Remediation.Recommendation: Review the agent's permission grants and remove any that are not essential for its function. Consider splitting the agent into multiple single-purpose agents with narrower scopes.

SourceNotes: Threshold of 5 permissions is a configurable heuristic based on least-privilege best practices.
